case_number: criminal revisional jurisdiction. Even otherwise no prejudice

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD Cr. Rev. Application No.S-32 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 07.04.2026 Mr. Pervez Ali advocate, holds brief for applicant’s counsel Mr. Tarique Ali Narai, advocate for private respondents Ms. Rameshan Oad, Deputy Prosecutor General *** Applicant Imam Bux has filed this criminal revision application against the Order dated 10.10.2025 passed by Special Judge CNS Matiari, whereby the subject FIR was disposed of under B-class. During course of arguments, the entire claim of the applicant in this lis is that he claims that he is the principal and lawful owner of Coure Car bearing registration No.AQS-453, Chassis No.L-501S- 7558317, Engine No.R-058645 Model 2008 and he is aggrieved by the impugned order to the extent that the police authorities have been directed to immediately handover the subject vehicle to its registered owner on production of original documents, under the valid receipt under intimation to trial Court. Heard counsel. Nothing is placed on record, which suggests that the applicant has filed any application before the competent Court concerning his claim that he in fact is the principal and lawful owner of the subject vehicle. It is common ground between parties that applicant is not the current owner. In fact during course of the arguments counsel for the applicant has conceded that applicant is not aggrieved by the order to the extent of disposal of the subject crime under B-class. The applicant’s grievance is simply that he has title in the vehicle and the vehicle is to be handed over to him. As such no cause of action or grievance is made out in this criminal revision application in terms of the order.

The applicant’s grievance is simply that he has title in the vehicle and the vehicle is to be handed over to him. As such no cause of action or grievance is made out in this criminal revision application in terms of the order. Per say the title and ownership cannot be decided by this Court while exercising the criminal revisional jurisdiction. Even otherwise no prejudice is caused to the applicant, if this criminal revision application is disposed of at this stage because the impugned order rightly directs the vehicle to be handed over to its owner and at this point, allegedly the car is yet to be transferred in the name of either applicant or the alleged accused/respondent. The applicant is at liberty to agitate his claim before proper forum. The impugned order does not call for any interference. Consequently, this criminal revision application stands dismissed for the above reasons. JUDGE JUDGE Sajjad Ali
